content:
===center
![voja1](https://pbs.twimg.com/media/D3UqdelWkAAMS0t.jpg)

*Tolsimir kept Voja's savagery in check.  With his Elven partner gone, he is but an exiled Conclave beast mourning his lost companion.*

*All that remains is his unrivaled fury.*
===endcenter

===accordion
===panel:Introduction & Gameplan

## Introduction & Gameplan

Voja was once the faithful companion of Tolsimir, a skillful elf warrior from the Selesnya Conclave.  The two fought side by side throughout many of Ravnica's pivotal conflicts and were so inseparable, in fact, that Voja never actually had his own card; instead always accompanying Tolsimir as a token ([[Tolsimir Wolfblood]], [[Tolsimir, Friend to Wolves]] and [[Tolsimir, Midnight's Light]]).

But this bond wasn't meant to last - as Tolsimir met his end during the events of Ravnica: Murders at Karlov Manor, fiercely defending his guild from accusations of murder.

Losing his lifelong companion, something vengeful was unleashed within, signified by the addition of Red mana into his otherwise Selesnya color identity.  While Voja's fury is boundless, his sense of loyalty to the Selesnyan elves remains.  This is reflected in his ability to buff all elves who stand by him when he attacks, leading them into a furious charge against his enemies.

Welcome everyone, to [[Voja, Jaws of the Conclave]].  This deck is built to take advantage of such a strong ability - effectively serving as a repeatable Elven [[Craterhoof Behemoth]] effect every turn.  Our elves, along with Voja himself, grow ever bigger and scarier.  We've leaned heavily into elf tribal to take advantage of ramp on bodies who give us the mana we need to cast our spells while also giving Voja the elves he needs to make the most of his ability.

Let's get into the gameplan:

===accordion
===panel:Early Game

Ramp as fast as possible, even if it means a creature-less board for a few turns.  We can take a bit of punishment early.  Our goal is to play [[Voja, Jaws of the Conclave]] as soon as possible.  Typically with commanders like this I would say wait until we've got a board that can capitalize on it but there are two reasons we are trying to turbo out Voja ASAP:

1. This deck has a ton of creature-based ramp and low-cost elves so odds are we'll have a couple of them anyway.

2. Voja even by himself can draw us at least one card each time he attacks so getting him out early will help us transition more quickly into the mid game.

===endpanel
===panel:Mid Game

Flood the board with elves so Voja's attack trigger will provide a massive buff to the whole team.  This deck has quite a few elves that bring utility that would otherwise be fulfilled by spells.  For example, [[Rocco, Cabaretti Caterer]] would almost certainly be [[Chord of Calling]] or [[Green Sun's Zenith]] in another deck, but in this one, it's all about having Elf bodies on board.

As for our other creatures, consider the board state and opponents' available mana.  The key to playing an aggressive creature-based deck is to not overcommit too hard, because vomiting our hand onto the field only for somebody to wipe the board feels really bad.  I'm not saying "hold 7+ cards in hand all the time" but consider playing a little conservatively if you don't have a board protection card such as [[Heroic Intervention]] or [[Teferi's Protection]] in hand.  Voja can buff the team and apply pressure even with just a few elves in play.

Continue hammering away at opponents' life totals while Voja draws cards and buffs the squad.

And remember to keep track of commander damage!  Voja has Trample will likely be huge around the mid game so it's not impossible to just take somebody out with commander damage.  Keep an eye on how much damage he has done to each opponent.  Often times you can put an opponent one Voja attack away from death and use that threat to force them to help you against the others.

===endpanel
===panel:Late Game

If the game hasn't ended, look to assemble one of two available combos in this deck that can decisively end the game.

The first is with [[Staff of Domination]] and a mana dork that can produce at least 5 mana.  The second is with [[The Red Terror]] and [[Shalai and Hallar]] or [[All Will Be One]].

We have [[Enlightened Tutor]], [[Archdruid's Charm]], [[Fauna Shaman]] and [[Rocco, Cabaretti Caterer]] to help assemble them.

Read up on the details in the **Combos** section of this primer.

===endpanel
===endaccordion

===endpanel
===panel:Pros, Cons & Power Level

## Pros, Cons & Power Level

**✅ Pros**

- This deck has an insane amount of ramp.  Due to the necessity of elves on board, it just so happens that a lot of them are mana dorks so you can play ahead of curve almost every game.

- Due to such a high ramp count, re-casting Voja if he gets removed becomes a trivial matter.

- This deck has multiple avenues of winning - combat damage, commander damage, burn damage or just a game-ending combo.

**❌ Cons**

- Being heavily reliant on creatures, it's highly susceptible to board wipes.  This is why we're running so many instant-speed protection cards.

- It's very reliant on its commander as well, because there aren't a lot of big mana sink spells/abilities outside of the odd Ezuri or Finale.  Voja really does need to be on board for this deck to feel powerful.

**☢️ Power Level**

- This deck is high-power casual.  Using WotC's bracket system, this deck is a 4.

- It primarily wants to win with combat damage via big, buffed creatures or commander damage, but it also has combos with a few tutors to help assemble them.

===endpanel
===panel:Packages

## Packages

===accordion
===panel:Ramp (29)

- [[Torgal, A Fine Hound]], [[Birds of Paradise]], [[Boreal Druid]], [[Elvish Mystic]], [[Fyndhorn Elves]], [[Llanowar Elves]], [[Bloom Tender]], [[Overgrown Zealot]], [[Paradise Druid]], [[Druid of the Anima]], [[Gyre Sage]], [[Incubation Druid]], [[Priest of Titania]], [[Circle of Dreams Druid]], [[Elvish Archdruid]], [[Marwyn, the Nurturer]], [[Selvala, Heart of the Wilds]], [[Sol Ring]] and [[Devoted Druid]] all produce mana on their own.

- [[Rishkar, Peema Renegade]] turns our creatures with +1/+1 counters into mana dorks.

- [[Cemetery Prowler]] reduces the cost of our spells.

- [[Baeloth Barrityl, Entertainer]] can make Treasure tokens we can exchange for mana.

- [[Wood Elves]], [[Farseek]], [[Nature's Lore]], [[Rampant Growth]], [[Three Visits]] and [[Archdruid's Charm]] can all get lands out of our deck and into play.

- [[Mutable Explorer]] creates a [[Mutavault]] token, effectively ramping us by 1 land.

===endpanel
===panel:Removal & Interaction (18)

- [[Annie Joins Up]] and [[All Will Be One]] can deal burn damage to creatures.

- [[Path to Exile]] and [[Swords to Plowshares]] can get rid of creatures.

- [[Bridgeworks Battle]] and [[Archdruid's Charm]] can force fights between creatures.

- [[Boseiju, Who Endures]], [[Beast Within]] and [[Generous Gift]] can get rid of most permanents.

- [[Aura Shards]] and [[Archdruid's Charm]] can deal with artifacts/enchantments.

- [[Selfless Safewright]] [[Inspiring Call]], [[Teferi's Protection]], [[Heroic Intervention]], [[Deflecting Swat]], [[Flawless Maneuver]] and [[Mithril Coat]] can be cast in response to opponents' removal to protect our stuff.

===endpanel
===panel:Card Draw & Card Advantage (10)

- [[Voja, Jaws of the Conclave]] is of course our main source of repeatable card draw.

- [[Leaf-Crowned Visionary]] and [[Beast Whisperer]] can draw us cards when we cast creatures.

- [[Staff of Domination]] has an activated ability that can draw us a card.

- [[Armorcraft Judge]] and [[Inspiring Call]] draw cards based on how many creatures with +1/+1 counters we have.

- [[Horizon Canopy]] and [[Sunbaked Canyon]] can be sacrificed to draw a card.

- [[Disciple of Freyalise]] can exchange of our creatures for card draw.

- [[Realmwalker]] isn't technically card draw but can let us cast elves off the top of our deck.

- [[Annie Joins Up]], [[Mirror Entity]] and [[Maskwood Nexus]] are not being added to the tally but they can work with Voja to draw even more cards.  [[Annie Joins Up]] doubles up Voja's trigger so we get double the +1/+1 counters AND doubles the number of cards drawn.  And the [[Mirror Entity]] and [[Maskwood Nexus]] can both turn our whole team into every creature type so Voja will see every creature as a wolf, resulting in way more card draw.

===endpanel
===endaccordion

===endpanel
===panel:Combos

## Combos

===accordion
===panel:Combo 1

===center
![combo1](https://i.imgur.com/4Pm6yUr.jpeg)
===endcenter

**Requirements**

- Any of the following mana dorks: [[Gyre Sage]], [[Priest of Titania]], [[Marwyn, the Nurturer]], [[Elvish Archdruid]], [[Circle of Dreams Druid]] or [[Selvala, Heart of the Wilds]].  

- Your mana dork of choice must be ready to tap (not summoning sick) and must net at least 5 net mana during this combo.  If you're using Selvala, she requires 1 mana to activate so she must make 6 with her ability.

- [[Staff of Domination]] on the field untapped.

**Steps**

1. Tap your dork to create at least 5 mana.

2. Spend [[symbol:3]] on [[Staff of Domination]]'s effect to untap that dork.

3. Spend [[symbol:1]] to untap [[Staff of Domination]].

4. You're now back to where you started except you've netted 1 mana.  Repeat steps 1-3 for infinite mana.

**After making infinite mana with steps 1-4,**

5. You can activate [[Staff of Domination]]'s card draw ability for [[symbol:5]], then untap it for [[symbol:1]] and repeat until you've drawn your [[Finale of Devastation]] and [[Ezuri, Renegade Leader]].  You can also keep going until you find [[Heroic Intervention]], [[Teferi's Protection]], [[Deflecting Swat]] and [[Flawless Maneuver]], since they can help you survive if this combo doesn't work out.  I would recommend leaving at least a couple of cards in your deck just in case.

6. With your infinite mana, go ahead and gain infinite life with [[Staff of Domination]] because why not?

7. Cast [[Allosaurus Shepherd]] if you have it, so your green spells can't be countered going forward.

8. Cast any selection of creatures you want, just be weary if you have [[Beast Whisperer]] on the field because its card draw effect is mandatory and you don't want to deck out.

9. Cast [[Ezuri, Renegade Leader]] and use his ability to grant Trample to all of your Elves.  If Ezuri is not available, you can get Trample from [[Bramblewood Paragon]] but you'll need Voja to grant those +1/+1 counters so be sure to cast him if you haven't yet.

10. Finally, cast [[Finale of Devastation]] for X = a trillion to give your whole team a power buff and Haste.

11. Move to combat and swing at your opponents to knock them all out via combat damage.

**Notes**

- Step 8 is completely optional.  You need at least 3 Elves for this combo to take out 3 opponents, and you should have them between [[Ezuri, Renegade Leader]], [[Allosaurus Shepherd]] and the mana dork from earlier.  3 infinite power Elves with Trample, one to take out each opponent.  That's all it takes but feel free to cast other creatures if you want.

- In Step 5, I'd recommend you keep drawing until you've found [[Heroic Intervention]], [[Teferi's Protection]], [[Inspiring Call]] and/or [[Flawless Maneuver]] because if this combo doesn't work out, you'll have to protect your board for one more rotation so you can try again next turn.

- Even if an opponent survives through this combo (with a [[Fog]] for example), you've got infinite life and multiple protection cards to hopefully save your board from any potential removal.  They would have to take you out with infect, commander damage or force you to empty your deck.  If they can't do that, the turn goes back to you and you can just repeat the infinite mana combo and sink it into [[Ezuri, Renegade Leader]]'s buff ability to win the game.

- Since this is a high power build, we have a few creature tutors ([[Fauna Shaman]], [[Archdruid's Charm]] and [[Rocco, Cabaretti Caterer]]) as well as [[Enlightened Tutor]] to help assemble this combo more consistently.

===endpanel
===panel:Combo 2

===center
![combo2](https://i.imgur.com/voKiqW6.jpeg)
===endcenter

**Requirements**

- [[Shalai and Hallar]] OR [[All Will Be One]] on the field.  We'll use [[All Will Be One]] in this example.

- [[The Red Terror]] on the field.

- Some way to put a +1/+1 counter on a creature you control (most of the time this will mean [[Voja, Jaws of the Conclave]] and at least one Elf).

**Steps**

1. Put a +1/+1 counter on one of your creatures, this triggers [[All Will Be One]], dealing damage to an opponent.

2. [[The Red Terror]] triggers, putting a +1/+1 counter on itself, which triggers [[All Will Be One]].

3. Repeat until all opponents are knocked out.

**Notes**

- It's not quite as simple as just two cards; you will need that initial way to grant a +1/+1 counter to get the combo started.

===endpanel
===endaccordion

===endpanel
===panel:No-Combo Kit

## No-Combo Kit

If your playgroup is not a fan of combos or you would just prefer to power this deck down a bit, you can remove the following cards:

- [[Staff of Domination]] - removing this disables combo 1

- [[The Red Terror]] - removing this disables combo 2

- [[Enlightened Tutor]] - this is mainly in here to help assemble combos and isn't really needed if you're taking combo pieces out

- [[Rocco, Cabaretti Caterer]] and [[Finale of Devastation]] - again, mainly for combo purposes and can be removed if you don't want them

With at least two open slots, I would just run [[Goblin Anarchomancer]] and [[Tolsimir, Midnight's Light]].

If more cards were removed, I would replace them with [[Rhythm of the Wild]], [[Taurean Mauler]] and [[Kutzil, Malamet Exemplar]].

===endpanel
===panel:Budget Options

## Budget Options

**Lands**

- The biggest chunk of money you can save is by simply replacing the expensive dual lands with cheaper ones that make the same kind of mana but enter tapped.  For example, [[Temple Garden]] becomes [[Canopy Vista]], [[Stomping Ground]] becomes [[Cinder Glade]], etc.  Just be sure to run at least a few of them with land types so you have some options to search for with things like [[Nature's Lore]] and [[Wood Elves]].

- [[Boseiju, Who Endures]] and [[Yavimaya, Cradle of Growth]] can both be replaced with basic [[Forest]]s.

- [[Arena of Glory]] can be replaced with a basic [[Mountain]].

- [[Prismatic Vista]] and [[Jetmir's Garden]] can be replaced with [[Exotic Orchard]] and [[Sheltering Landscape]].

**Enchantments**

- [[Aura Shards]] can be replaced with [[Rhythm of the Wild]].

- [[All Will Be One]] is a combo piece but if you don't want to run it, [[Tolsimir, Friend to Wolves]] can take its place.

**Artifacts**

- [[Mithril Coat]] can be replaced with [[Anara, Wolvid Familiar]].

**Instants**

- [[Enlightened Tutor]] can be replaced with [[Moon-Blessed Cleric]] or [[Nature's Claim]].

- [[Heroic Intervention]] can be replaced with [[Unbreakable Formation]] or [[Stroke of Midnight]].

- [[Deflecting Swat]] can be replaced with [[Bolt Bend]] or [[Chaos Warp]].

- [[Teferi's Protection]] can be replaced with [[Boros Charm]] or [[Clever Concealment]].

**Sorceries**

- [[Finale of Devastation]] is part of a combo but if you don't want to run it, you can play [[Chord of Calling]] or a board wipe like [[Blasphemous Act]] or [[Farewell]] instead.

**Creatures**

- [[Allosaurus Shepherd]] can be replaced with [[Copperhorn Scout]] or [[Elvish Rejuvenator]].

- [[Selvala, Heart of the Wilds]] can be replaced with [[Goblin Anarchomancer]].

**Future Cards**

- [[Mutable Explorer]] has not yet released in paper.  If you'd like a substitute, I'd suggest [[Dionus, Elvish Archdruid]].

===endpanel
===panel:Change Log

## Change Log

===accordion
===panel:6/1/24

- [[Forest]] and [[Mountain]] were replaced with [[Disciple of Freyalise]] and [[Arena of Glory]], respectively.

- [[Rhythm of the Wild]] was removed in favor of [[Lightning Greaves]] because it grants Voja haste AND protection.

- [[Wirewood Channeler]] was removed because we have 7 other mana dorks that can combo so we can afford to cut this one.  [[Evolution Witness]] was added for some repeatable recursion.

===endpanel
===panel:3/31/24

- [[Roaming Throne]] was replaced with [[Annie Joins Up]] because it also doubles up Voja triggers but also is much harder to remove.

===endpanel
===panel:3/11/24

- [[Elvish Champion]] was removed because a +1/+1 buff wasn't really impactful considering Voja is granting much bigger buff.  It was replaced with [[Mirror Entity]] because it can turn all creatures into all types so we get a big buff + big draw when we swing.

===endpanel
===panel:2/18/24

- Had to cut [[The Great Henge]] because I needed it for another deck and it's not *needed* in this deck.  Good for sure, not necessary.  Also I wanted to make room for a Haste enabler so [[Rhythm of the Wild]] was brought in.

- [[Canopy Tactician]] was also cut because I want my mana dorks to cost 3 or less mana for the purposes of getting Voja out early.  [[Canopy Tactician]] doesn't do that.  If I'm in a position to cast this, then next turn I will have enough to cast Voja normally, so [[Canopy Tactician]] didn't help me get the big wolf out.  Make sense?  For that reason, it was replaced with [[Viridian Joiner]] because it's a cheaper mana dork AND it's another combo piece with [[Staff of Domination]].

===endpanel
===endaccordion

===endpanel
===endaccordion
